input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 targeting Android + Windows:

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK (latest with MAUI support)

```powershell
# Discover latest SDK version
Invoke-RestMethod https://dotnetcli.azureedge.net/dotnet/Sdk/LTS/latest.version

# Install via winget
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.8
```

## 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to confirm name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (includes android, ios stubs, etc.)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Android SDK Dependencies

The MAUI workload installs Android SDK components, but you may need to manage versions:

```powershell
# Find where the Android SDK was placed
$androidSdk = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk"
# Or check:
dotnet android sdk info

# List installed Android platforms/build-tools
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list

# Install specific API level (discover latest stable dynamically)
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "platforms;android-34" `
  "build-tools;34.0.0" `
  "platform-tools"

# Accept all licenses
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses
```

## 4. Set Up Android Emulator (Optional)

```powershell
# Install emulator + system image
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "emulator" `
  "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create AVD
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
  -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64" --device "pixel_6"
```

## 5. Enable Windows App SDK (for Windows target)

```powershell
# Comes with the maui workload, but confirm:
dotnet workload list | Select-String "maui-windows"

# If missing:
dotnet workload install maui-windows
```

## 6. Enable Developer Mode (required for Windows target)

```powershell
# Enable Developer Mode via registry
reg add "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /t REG_DWORD /f /v "AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense" /d "1"
```

## 7. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (if not using full VS)

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools

# Then add required components:
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" modify `
  --installPath "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\BuildTools" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.XamarinBuildTools `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.Maui.All `
  --quiet
```

## 8. Verify Everything

```powershell
# Check all dependencies in one shot
dotnet maui check

# Create and build a test project
dotnet new maui -n TestApp
cd TestApp

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0
```

## 9. Discover Target Framework Versions Dynamically

```powershell
# List available TFMs for your SDK
dotnet --list-sdks

# See supported platforms in a MAUI template
dotnet new maui --dry-run | Select-String "TargetFrameworks"

# Query NuGet for latest MAUI package version
Invoke-RestMethod "https://api.nuget.org/v3-flatcontainer/microsoft.maui.controls/index.json" |
  Select-Object -ExpandProperty versions | Select-Object -Last 5
```

---

**Key notes:**
- `dotnet maui check` is your single best diagnostic command — run it after setup to catch missing pieces.
- Replace `net8.0` with `net9.0` if you install .NET 9 SDK instead.
- Hyper-V or WHPX must be enabled for hardware-accelerated Android emulation (`Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All`).
